title: Calling all Data Engineers in Product-Based Companies
content:
Hi everyone!

I see a lot of discussions around Software Engineering interview experiences and compensation, but not enough for Data Engineering roles.

If you’ve recently interviewed for a Data Engineer position at a product-based company, it would be super helpful if you could share:

• Company name 
• Years of experience
• Interview rounds/process
• Tech stack/topics asked
• Compensation offered/current CTC
• Overall interview experience & difficulty level

I’m planning to switch soon, and I believe this thread could help many other Data Engineers preparing for the market as well.

Would really appreciate your insights 🙌

title: Should i quit? 11 months of unemployment?
content:
Hi everyone,
I dont know whether this post will reach people out there or not, but I genuinely need help. I am a 2024 grad, after my graduation I joined a PBC as SWE-I. My role got impacted last year july due to business decisions. I am constantly practicing LC for problem solving, doing side projects to keep my dev skills and GenAI part all fresh... but it's been 11 months, I am jobless... I dont want to be a burden to my family, I am living on my savings, which is near to a end.
I only got 6-7 interviews in this 11 months, 3 ghosted me...1 offer got revoked at the last moment. I dont know where I am losing this game...I am really exhausted, I really want to quit...I am losing all of me.

I am not here for sympathy, i genuinely need insights and I want suggestions...please do consider me as your close friend, and please help me in this....

I have 1 YOE and 4 months of internship experience.
I am proficient in Java/SpringBoot, React.js , TypeScript, JavaScript, Go, Node.js.
I have exposure working with MySQL, BigQuery, GCP too.
My max contest rating is - 1817

any suggestions will be really helpful for me, trust me I am really in verge of giving up, so if you can guide me i'll be forever thankful.

Thank you

title: SSE (Backend) @ Sprinklr interview experience
content:
I recently gave interview at Sprinklr for SSE role backend. The process consists of 3 rounds total (All three techincal)

# Round 1: DSA -> 1hr
**Problem**:
```
Find the total distinct subsequence possible in an array A such that a[i] - a[j] = (i-j) * d
```

Was able to solve this after a little hint. You can try doing some math around the above equation and can solve it.

Verdict: Hire

# Round 2: DSA + System design -> 1hr
This was one of the best round I have given, how interviewer carried this whole problem was interesting. 
**Problem**: This was the question: https://leetcode.com/discuss/post/685338/microsoft-onsite-design-the-t9-predictiv-ef6a/

PS: Didn't knew this feature exists in all phone, discovered in the interview :)

Was able to suggest a typical trie structure based solution and then discussed how we can modify the standard trie structure to search contacts more efficiently.

Later, I was asked how I would productionize this system and what the table schema would look like.

Verdict: Strong hire

# Round 3: Hiring manager -> 1hr
This was the first system design round I had given. I was not able to structure my answer properly and panicked a lot because I was unfamiliar with the system. Somehow, I was able to design a working solution, but it was not the most optimal one.

I was also not communicating with the interviewer throughout the discussion. I explained the design only after completing it entirely. I think that was the point where things went wrong.

**Problem**:
```
Channel: FB, IG, TW..
Partners: Microsoft, Dell, Amazon, Google
Accounts: DellFB, AmazonTW, GoogleTK..

Millions of messages across thousands of these accounts daily

Design a scalable Alert Aggregation and Notification System for a social media message ingestion pipeline at Sprinklr.

Monitoring failures
Deduplication of alerts
Escalation policy
Auto Recovery

```
Verdict: No hire
After this round, I received feedback from the HR stating that they would not be moving forward with my application. However, detailed feedback was not provided.

**PLEASE UPVOTE!!**

Would really appreciate insights from people who interviewed/offered recently for Kotak SDE roles or similar fintech companies.

title: Snowflake OA Hackerrank 3 coding question
content:
Snowflake Online Assessment as a 90-minute HackerRank test with 3 coding questions, usually around Easy-Medium, Medium, and Medium-Hard difficulty. It says the main focus areas are dynamic programming, string processing, greedy algorithms, priority queues, and careful boundary-case handling. The article emphasizes that Snowflake OA questions often have long descriptions, strict edge cases, and high time pressure.

The high-frequency question types mentioned include Prime String Partition, which uses DP to count valid ways to split a numeric string into prime-number substrings; Text Scoring / Prefix Match Score, which can be optimized with KMP or prefix matching; Constrained Vowel Strings, a DP problem counting strings with limited consecutive vowels; Task Scheduling with Constraints, usually solved with sorting plus a priority queue; and String Rotation Check, where the classic trick is checking whether target appears in src + src.

For preparation, the article recommends focusing heavily on DP counting problems and state compression, practicing string algorithms like KMP, and doing full 90-minute HackerRank-style mock tests. It also suggests time allocation: finish the first problem in about 20 minutes, spend 25–30 minutes on the second, and leave enough time for the hardest third problem. Boundary cases such as empty strings, single characters, leading zeros, repeated characters, and overlap cases are highlighted as especially important.

More content can be referred to https://reurl.cc/18qGb8

title: Vegapay Offer Evaluation | SDE-2 | Lowballed? + WLB Check
content:
Hi Everyone,

I recently received an **SDE-2** offer from **Vegapay** with a total compensation of **25 LPA (Base + Variable + Stocks)**.
Based on my research, they have recently been hiring folks from **MAANG and Tier-1 companies**, where even an SDE-1 typically makes a 25 LPA base. It seems highly unlikely that these candidates would jump to a startup without a premium, which makes me suspect I am being significantly lowballed given this is my total CTC.

### Questions:
 1. **Compensation:** Is 25 LPA (Base + Variable + Stocks) the standard for an SDE-2 at Vegapay, or am I being lowballed? What does their current internal salary band look like for this role?
  2. **WLB & Culture:** How is the work-life balance and overall engineering culture at the company right now?
  

Would appreciate any insights from current employees or anyone who has recently negotiated with them. Thanks!

title: [Offer Discussion] JP Morgan Chase SDE 2 — 29L Fixed + 12% Bonus | Worth it?
content:
Hey everyone, wanted to get some honest opinions here before making a decision.

My Profile
Education : BTech Tier 1 (Delhi)
YOE : 4 years
Prior Experience : Product based company (Healthcare tech)
Current CTC : 20L fixed, no variable
Current Situation : Fully remote, good WLB, living with family

The Offer
Company : JP Morgan Chase
Level : SDE 2 (Grade 601)
Location : Bangalore (4 days WFO)
Fixed : 29L
Bonus : 10-12% variable (verbally confirmed)
Stock : None
Signing Bonus : None
Total Comp : ~32-33L

My Dilemma
Currently on a remote job with great WLB and zero expenses living with family. Moving to Bangalore means easily 20-25K monthly just on rent and transport.
So effectively my savings won't grow much despite the hike on paper.
On the flip side JPMC brand is strong and fintech at this scale is genuinely exciting. Also tried pushing for SDE 3 basis my 4 YOE but they said band is fixed at 601.

My Questions

Is this switch worth it financially given the Bangalore expenses?
Anyone who joined JPMC at similar comp ,how was the growth and internal promotion timeline?
Is SDE 2 to SDE 3 promotion realistic within 1-2 years internally?
